2605.19781.title: From SGD to Muon: Adaptive Optimization via Schatten-p Norms
2605.19781.paper_summary: Modern optimizers, like Muon, impose matrix-wise geometry constraints on their updates. These matrix-wise constraints can be unified under Linear Minimization Oracle (LMO) theory. However, all current methods impose fixed LMO geometries for the update rules, chosen by-design or empirically, which are not necessarily optimal according to the problem's geometry. We introduce a novel efficient datadriven criterion for dynamically choosing proxy-optimal update LMO geometries on individual Deep Neural Network layers. Derived in closed form from gradient and activation statistics using a single-step random feature regression surrogate model, our criterion navigates a design space interpolating from SGD to Muon updates. Moreover, integrating parameter-wise preconditioning allows our framework to recover SGD, Muon, Adam, and MuAdam as specific extrema. To make this adaptive approach scalable, we pair it with efficient computational strategies, achieving only a $\sim$ 3% runtime overhead on highly optimized baselines. As a proof of concept, we show that this data-driven optimizer beats or remains competitive with the performance of the best performing optimizer between Muon and AdamW across three different training scenarios. Ultimately, this work provides evidence that LMO geometry can be successfully and efficiently adapted from runtime data, opening a new pathway for optimizer design beyond static geometries.
2605.19781.paper_summary_zh: 現代優化器，如Muon，對其更新施加矩陣級幾何約束。這些矩陣級約束可以統一在線性最小化預言機（LMO）理論下。然而，所有當前方法對更新規則施加固定的LMO幾何，這些幾何是根據設計或經驗選擇的，並不一定根據問題的幾何是最佳的。我們引入了一種新穎的高效數據驅動標準，用於動態選擇個別深度神經網絡層的代理最優更新LMO幾何。該標準是通過使用單步隨機特徵回歸替代模型從梯度和激活統計中以封閉形式推導而來，我們的標準在設計空間中插值，從SGD到Muon更新。此外，集成參數級預處理使我們的框架能夠恢復SGD、Muon、Adam和MuAdam作為特定的極值。為了使這種自適應方法可擴展，我們將其與高效計算策略相結合，在高度優化的基線上僅實現約3%的運行時開銷。作為概念驗證，我們展示了這種數據驅動的優化器在三種不同的訓練場景中超越或保持與Muon和AdamW之間最佳表現優化器的競爭力。最終，這項工作提供了證據，表明LMO幾何可以成功且高效地從運行時數據中適應，為超越靜態幾何的優化器設計開辟了一條新途徑。
2605.19781.author: Thomas Massena et.al.
2605.19781.authors: Thomas Massena, Corentin Friedrich, Mathieu Serrurier
